title: Unsupported Uniface Packing Codes
text: Unsupported Uniface Packing Codes It is possible that Uniface supports a connector-level packing code that does not have a corresponding storage format in your DBMS. For example, some DBMSs have no support for Dates and Times. You can resolve this by: Mapping the packing code to one of the ASCII String packing codes If you decide to redefine the packing code to a packing code that does have a corresponding storage format, this is not expected to be a problem, since all packing codes can be redefined to an ASCII String packing code. This is even true for the Raw packing codes, since they can be redefined to Uniface TRX format (TRX is the Uniface transfer format that is both platform and DBMS independent, suitable even for Raw data). Convert data in the connector to map the packing code to an alternative storage format, using the data manipulation connector requests. In this case, the connector chooses a storage format in the application model that corresponds to the packing code and handles format conversions in the data manipulation connector requests. CAUTION: This is an extremely complicated approach; there are no connector service functions available to help you carry out the conversion, so be warned that this solution is your responsibility. Related Links Database Connector Interface The Database Connector Interface is an API for building custom database connectors. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
